output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's What to Do (Legally)
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can deduct, and how to demand your money back in 60 seconds.
content_outline: Why Landlords Withhold Security Deposits (And When It's Illegal)
What Your Landlord Can and Cannot Deduct From Your Deposit
How to Write a Security Deposit Demand Letter That Actually Works
State-by-State Deadlines: How Long Is Too Long?
Next Steps If Your Landlord Still Won't Return Your Deposit
key_points: Most states require landlords to return deposits within 14-30 days or provide an itemized deduction list
Normal wear and tear cannot be legally deducted from a security deposit
A formal demand letter is the fastest, most effective first step before small claims court
Tenants do not need a lawyer to send a legally sound demand letter
Deposits-Back.com generates your state-specific demand letter in 60 seconds for a flat $19.99 with no sign-up or subscription
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate a legally sound security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds — just $19.99, no sign-up or subscription required.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters who have moved out and are waiting for a security deposit return, or have been told their deposit is being withheld for questionable damages, cleaning fees, or unpaid rent.
